features: Swiss Cheese plants are an attractive and easy to grow indoor houseplant known for its eye catching leathery and glossy heart shaped leaves which become perforated and split when mature
A great entry plant for household gardeners Monstera Deliciosa is relatively easy to care for, and makes a great statement piece in your living room.
Monstera are evergreen indoor plants and prefer indirect sunlight
This houseplant will thrive in well drained, moderately moist soil.
Supplied in a 17cm nursery pot and approximately 60 cm high. The decorative pot is not included. This plant will be supplied in a growers pot.
description: Product Description How to grow Monstera are evergreen plants and prefer indirect sunlight. Too much direct light in warmer months may burn the leaves. Monstera thrive in well drained, moderately moist soil. During the growing season, water every one to two weeks. Water until excess drains through. Monstera produce large volumes of aerial roots which can grow out of the pot and get out of control. These can be trimmed when needed. Swiss Cheeseplant Monstera Deliciosa Monstera Deliciosa , more commonly known as a Swiss Cheeseplant or Split leaf philodendrum, this is an attractive and easy to grow houseplant known for its eye catching leathery and glossy heart shaped leaves which become perforated and split when mature. Native to Central America this plant can grow to over a metre tall when mature. This is a great entry plant for household gardener and is relatively easy to care for. Its eye catching leaves make a great statement in any living room. This variety has large heart shaped leaves which split when mature This variety has large heart shaped leaves which split when mature Native to the tropical rainforests of Panama and Mexico the plant developed ribbon like leaves to allow light and water to the roots below Perfect Plants for any room House Plants Indoor plants are an integral part of modern living, adding beauty, health, and a touch of nature to our indoor spaces. Whether you're an experienced plant enthusiast or just starting to develop your green thumb, incorporating a small or large house plant into your home is a delightful and rewarding experience that brings joy and harmony to your daily life. So, why not invite a few green companions into your living space and let them work their magic? Air Purifying Plants House plants are not only aesthetically pleasing but also contribute to creating a healthier indoor environment. They act as natural air purifiers, removing pollutants and toxins from the air while releasing oxygen, making the atmosphere fresher and more pleasant to breathe. Their ability to absorb carbon dioxide and emit oxygen helps to regulate humidity levels, reducing the dryness often associated with air-conditioned spaces.
